findings.evidence: Line 58 `@[simp] lemma mem_completeLinearSystem` rewrites the same LHS unconditionally; line 167's binder `{w : X → ℤ}` occurs only in `hw`, `h`, `hD`, not in `E ∈ S.completeLinearSystem D ↔ E = 0 ∧ S.divisorClass D = 0`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[simp]`; keep it as a plain characteristic lemma applied explicitly (`simp only [...]` / `rw`). It cannot serve as a global normal-form rule with an undetermined `w`.
findings.issue: @[simp] on mem_completeLinearSystem_iff_eq_zero_and_divisorClass_eq_zero_of_weightedDegree_zero has LHS `E ∈ S.completeLinearSystem D`, identical to the pre-existing unconditional `@[simp] mem_completeLinearSystem` (line 58). Worse, the weight `w` appears only in the hypotheses (hw/h/hD), never in the conclusion, so simp has no way to instantiate `w` and this lemma can never fire as a rewrite — the annotation is dead.

findings.evidence: Both line 58 and line 282 carry `@[simp]` with head pattern `_ ∈ S.completeLinearSystem _`; line 58 fires with no side conditions while line 282 requires discharging `IsUnweightedDegreeZero` and `degree D = 0`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[simp]` and use the lemma explicitly. A specialized degree-zero normal form cannot coexist as a global simp rule with the unconditional membership-unfolding simp lemma already in scope.
findings.issue: @[simp] on mem_completeLinearSystem_iff_eq_zero_and_divisorClass_eq_zero_of_degree_zero competes with the unconditional `@[simp] mem_completeLinearSystem` (line 58) for the same LHS `E ∈ S.completeLinearSystem D`. The unconditional lemma reduces membership to the generic effective+equivalent shape and pre-empts this conditional one, so the intended rigidity normal form is not reliably produced and simp behaviour on this LHS becomes order-dependent.

summary: The characteristic rigidity API (membership/nonempty/singleton, both class and linear-equivalence forms, weighted and unweighted) is complete and bodies stay hidden, but the two new @[simp] annotations are wrong: they collide with the existing unconditional simp normal form for the same LHS, and the weighted one cannot fire at all.
